changes_made: Renamed k8s_rbac_critical_asset_identification to k8s_rbac_critical_asset_access_control for clarity
review_notes: The control CP-2 (8) is applicable to Kubernetes as it involves identifying and protecting critical assets, which can be mapped to Kubernetes security features. The original function names mostly follow the naming conventions, but 'k8s_rbac_critical_asset_identification' was slightly misleading as RBAC is more about access control rather than identification. The other functions are well-named and map correctly to Kubernetes security controls.
naming_improvements: Ensure RBAC-related functions emphasize access control rather than asset identification
title: Contingency Plan | Identify Critical Assets
step1_reasoning: The control CP-2 (8) focuses on identifying and protecting critical assets. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to identifying critical workloads and ensuring their protection through various security measures. RBAC can be used to control access to critical assets, etcd backups ensure data resilience, NetworkPolicies protect network access to critical assets, and labeling pods can help in identifying and managing critical workloads.
